State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance
(916) 322-5662

322-5660

322-5901

322-6441

•

Statement of Economic Interest
322-6444

February 19, 1985


Effie McDermott
166 Leeward Avenue
Pismo Beach, CA 93449

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our No. A-85-015

Dear Ms. McDermott:

You have written requesting our advice based upon the
following facts.

FACTS

In 1982, I ran for an office and had to loan my
committee funds. When I closed out the reports, the
balance of the personal loan reverted to a contribution due to inability of the committee to repay same.

In 1984, I again ran for the same office and filed
again as a committee with a new number. The 1984
campaign closed with a surplus in the account.

QUESTION

Can the 1984 Committee make a contribution to the
1982 Committee and repay part of the unpaid loan?

ANSWER

If the 1982 Committee terminated and closed its books,
showing no remaining obligations, then there is no committee to
which the 1984 Committee may contribute. See Government Code
Section 84214 and Commission regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18404. However, if you showed the loan as an outstanding
obligation in the 1982 Committee's final statement, a
contribution may be made to the 1982 Committee for the purpose
of repaying the debt. Of course, a report must be filed. As to
whether the loan may otherwise be paid off from the 1984

Effie McDermott
February 19, 1985
Page 2


Committee, we suggest you consult with the Attorney General's
Office as that agency, not ours, oversees the "personal use" law
in Elections Code Sections 12400, et seq

Sincerely,

Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel
Legal Division

January 16, 1985

State of California
1100 Kay Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Gentlemen:

Please answer this question for me:

In 1982, I ran for an office and had to loan my committee
funds. When I closed out the reports, the balance of the
personal loan reverted to a contribution due to inability of
the committee to repay same.
In 1984, I again ran for the same office and filed again
as a committee with a new number. The 1984 campaign closed
with a surplus in the account.

Question:
Can the 1984 Committee make a contribution to the 1982
Committee and repay part of the unpaid loan?

Thank you for your assistance.

Yours truly,

Effie McDermott
166 Leeward Ave.
Pismo Beach, CA 93449

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660        322-5901        322-6441        322-6444

January 29, 1985


Effie McDermott
166 Leeward Avenue
Pismo Beach, CA  93449

                                                'Re: A-85-015

Dear Ms. McDermott:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been referred to Robert E. Leidigh, an
attorney in the Legal Division of the Fair Political
Practices Commission.  If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact this attorney directly at
(916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [illegible]
                                        Barbara A. Milman
                                        General Counsel
